Normal: An image of the object in its normal condition without any defects.
Structural Damage: An image showing structural damage including cracks, breaks, or deformation altering the object's original shape.
Surface Defect: An image revealing surface anomalies such as scratches, dents, or discoloration contrasting with intact areas.
Material Contamination: An image displaying foreign material contamination like stains, particles, or liquid residues on the surface.
Assembly Error: An image depicting missing components, misplaced parts, or orientation errors in object assembly.
Functional Failure: An image demonstrating functional failures including bent connectors or broken mechanisms.
Combined Defects: An image containing multiple coexisting defect types requiring comprehensive inspection.
